title: Government Budget — Iran in Charts (Stanford Iran 2040 Project)
source_org: Stanford University, Hamid and Christina Moghadam Program in Iranian Studies — Iran 2040 Project

files.name: government_budget_dashboard_full_2026-07-12.png
files.original_name: 1.png (Tableau Public static render, served as 1_rss.png too)

time_coverage: approx. 1998-2018 (Khatami through Rouhani administrations; 2018 spending breakdown for a single year)
units: Constant 2016 USD billions or billion Rial (toggle in original interactive viz); percent of total for normalized charts; percent of GDP for the ratios chart
license_terms: No explicit license stated; dashboard page footer states: 'By using the presented data or any presentations of data derived from them, you consent to be bound by the following terms of use. The data presented here is solely based on the data available in the public domain and we are not responsible for the accuracy and usefulness of any of the presented data.' Treat as research/non-commercial use; not redistributable as a commercial product.
notes:
The live dashboard at the source_url is a Drupal page (iranian-studies.stanford.edu) embedding a Tableau Public visualization ('GovernmentBudget/Budget', published by profile stanford.iran.2040.project). A direct WebFetch to the Drupal page returns HTTP 403 (bot-check); it renders fine in a real browser. Citation shown on the dashboard itself: 'Pooya Azadi, Matin Mirramezani, Technical Note: Iran in Charts, Stanford Iran 2040 Project, 2022.' 'Iran in Charts' is the umbrella name for a family of ~9 Stanford Iran 2040 dashboards (Population, Energy, International Trade, Social and Criminal, Agriculture and Food, Health, Housing, Monetary Data, Government Budget, Income and Pensions); no separate downloadable PDF/CSV technical note specifically for the budget dashboard was found on the Publications page — the dashboard itself IS the publication. The related free PDF 'Iran's Large and Growing Financing Gap' (Azadi & Mirramezani, Sept 2019) was already captured separately in data/raw/stanford-iran-2040/stanford-iran-2040-archive/ and discusses the same fiscal-deficit theme narratively. The authors' 2022 Stanford University Press book 'The Struggle for Development in Iran' likely contains the full underlying budget data/table but is a paywalled monograph, not freely downloadable, and was not pursued per source-hygiene/paywall norms.

DOWNLOAD METHOD: In-browser, clicking the viz's 'Download' button (which offers Image/Data/Crosstab/PDF/PowerPoint/Workbook in standard Tableau Public UI) triggers an AWS WAF CAPTCHA ('Security verification' modal, class _awsWafCaptchaModal_) — per project rules, CAPTCHA/bot-detection bypass is prohibited, so that route was abandoned without attempting to solve it. Tableau Public tooltips (which would give exact per-year values) also did not trigger via automated mouse hover in this session. INSTEAD, the dataset file here was obtained via Tableau Public's standard static PNG export, which every published Tableau Public viz exposes as a plain, non-interactive public asset (used for its social-share/gallery thumbnail) at https://public.tableau.com/static/images/Go/GovernmentBudget/Budget/1.png (and identically at .../1_rss.png) — fetched with a plain curl + browser User-Agent, HTTP 200, no CAPTCHA, no login. This is NOT a bypass of any protection; it is the vendor's own public static asset endpoint. The PNG (1200x2027px) renders the FULL dashboard at good resolution: all 6 charts, their legends, axis gridlines, AND actual x-axis year labels (1996/1998 through 2016/2018) that are only visible in this full static render, not in the cropped live-viewport screenshots taken earlier in the session.

CHART-BY-CHART DESCRIPTION (read visually off the gridlines in the saved PNG; treated as approximate visual reads, NOT exact source figures, since no data export or working tooltip was available):
1. 'Government Public Budget Revenue' (top-left): stacked bar chart, y-axis 'Constant 2016 $B or B Rial' 0-100, x-axis individual years ~2001-2016 grouped under three column headers Khatami | Ahmadinejad | Rouhani (administration periods, not exact term boundaries drawn precisely). Stack categories bottom-to-top: Oil (black), Tax (red), Others (orange), Deficit (yellow). Visually: oil revenue rises from ~13 (2001) to a local peak ~25 (mid-2000s), a sharp spike to ~45 around 2008 and again ~45 around 2011 (black bars visibly taller in those two years), then drops back to ~17-20 under Rouhani (2013-2016). Total bar height (revenue) peaks near 97 around 2007-2008 (Ahmadinejad), dips under Rouhani to a low ~44 around 2013, then partially recovers to ~78 by 2016.
2. 'Government Public Budget Expenditure' (top-right): same axis/x-groups, stack categories Consumption (dark green), Investment (light green), Financial Assets (yellow). Expenditure closely tracks the revenue pattern (near-balanced budget by construction with 'Deficit'/gap shown separately), peaking ~93 around 2008-2010 under Ahmadinejad (driven mostly by Consumption ~65-70), dropping to ~41 low around 2012-2013, recovering to ~78 by 2016 (Consumption ~57).
3. 'Sources of Government Revenue' (mid-left): same categories as chart 1 but normalized to 100% (percent-of-total), x-axis years ~1996/1998-2018. Oil's revenue SHARE is highest early (~57% around 2001-2002) and again spikes to ~49% around 2008 and ~49% around 2011, falls to a low ~19% share around 2013 (Rouhani, sanctions-driven oil-export collapse), recovering only partially to ~26-33% by 2016-2017 — i.e., the dashboard visually documents Iran's post-2012 sanctions shift toward non-oil (mostly Tax) revenue reliance.
4. 'Government Spending in 2018' (mid-right): horizontal bar chart, single year (2018) functional breakdown, x-axis 'Share of Spending (%)' 0-25, stacked by Financial Assets/Investment/Consumption where applicable. Approximate shares read off the gridlines: Social Welfare ~24%, Education ~16%, Defence ~14%, Other Uses ~12%, Financial Assets ~9%, Economic (Affairs) ~8%, Health ~7%, Public Services ~3%, Culture/Sport/Tourism ~3%, Judiciary ~3%, Housing ~1.5%, Environment ~0.2%.
5. 'Tax Revenue (constant 2016 $B)' (bottom-left): stacked bar, y-axis 0-35, x-axis years ~2001-2016 under Khatami/Ahmadinejad/Rouhani headers, categories Corporate (darkest red), Goods and Services, Income, Import, Wealth Tax (lightest) — tax revenue grows from ~7 (2001) to a peak ~33 around 2008-2010, dips to ~16 around 2012-2013, recovers to ~27 by 2016.
6. 'Tax and Government Spending to GDP Ratios' (bottom-right): line chart, y-axis '%' 0-25, x-axis years ~1996/1998-2018, three series: Government Expenditure/GDP (red-pink line, ~17% in early 2000s, peaks ~23% around 2005-2008, dips to a low ~14% around 2012-2013, rises back to ~22% by 2016-2017), Tax Revenue/GDP (light teal, comparatively flat/stable ~5-8% across the whole period), Government Investment/GDP (dark green, ~3-7% through the 2000s, declines sharply after ~2010 to ~2-3%, slight uptick by 2016).

Controls seen but not exercised (to avoid further destabilizing the shared browser session): a 'Gregorian Calendar' dropdown (implies a Persian/Solar Hijri calendar toggle option), a 'Show Historical Events' dropdown (implies annotated historical-event overlays), and a 'Constant 2016 $B' unit dropdown on charts 1/2/5 (implies a toggle to nominal Billion Rial). These could reveal additional views/precision in a future session.

CAVEAT: all numeric values above are approximate visual reads off chart gridlines in a compressed PNG, not verified against source data or tooltips. Treat as order-of-magnitude / qualitative-trend information only. Do not cite specific numbers from this manifest as precise budget figures without independent verification against a primary Iranian government source (e.g. Plan & Budget Organization budget laws, already collected separately under iran-plan-budget-org).

failures.result: HTTP 403 (bot-check); resolved by using the browser preview tool instead, which rendered the page fine.

failures.attempted: In-viz 'Download' button on the embedded Tableau Public visualization (https://public.tableau.com/views/GovernmentBudget/Budget), to get the underlying data/crosstab/workbook
failures.result: Triggers an AWS WAF 'Security verification' CAPTCHA modal. Not attempted further per project rules against bypassing bot-detection/CAPTCHAs. No underlying data table, workbook, or crosstab was obtained.

failures.attempted: Hovering over individual bars in the live rendered viz to trigger Tableau's native mark tooltips (which would show exact year + value)
failures.result: No tooltip appeared under automated mouse hover in this session (possibly requires canvas-level focus/click-to-activate behavior not reproduced by the automation, or cross-origin iframe event handling). Exact per-mark values were therefore not obtainable; see qualitative/approximate description in notes instead.

failures.attempted: Searching Stanford Iran 2040 Publications page for a standalone 'Iran in Charts' PDF/CSV technical note with the full underlying budget data table
failures.result: No such standalone file found; 'Iran in Charts' is the name of the dashboard collection itself (9 dashboard topics), not a separate downloadable document. The closest free related PDF ('Iran's Large and Growing Financing Gap', 2019) was already captured in data/raw/stanford-iran-2040/stanford-iran-2040-archive/. The authors' 2022 Stanford UP book likely has the full data table but is a paywalled monograph and was not pursued.
